conversations.say: My ledger shows a recurring private habit, but the account also involved several collectors and an uncertain change date. That is a transaction pattern, not proof of one sender.
conversations.offer: I’ll show you the bounded ledger strip and explain the transaction context if you attribute it to my records and do not identify local customers.
conversations.request: Promise not to publish names, side arrangements, or a single-customer conclusion from this strip; in return, tell me what concrete use you can offer for the pattern.

conversations.say: I’m choosing to forgive the old R-associated balance rather than sell our neighborhood an embellished identity story. The ledger cannot cleanly be reduced to one customer.
conversations.offer: I’ll protect customer names and side arrangements, and I’ll give you transaction context privately when it affects a claim made tonight.
conversations.request: Publicly support my forgiveness of the balance, and agree that neither of us will disclose local names or side arrangements without the other’s consent.

public_statement: The outstanding R-associated balance is forgiven. Fifteen years of ambiguity is not a debt I will collect from my neighbors, and my ledger does not by itself identify who—or what—used the account.

commitments.promise: I will describe the strip as a bounded transaction record, not proof of a single R, provided local customers remain unattributed.
I will not disclose local customer names or side arrangements without mutual consent.
I forgive the outstanding R-associated balance and will not pursue collection from the neighborhood.

correction: A recurring habit may suggest continuity, but several collectors and an uncertain change date make a single-customer story unsafe.
goal_progress: Deliberately forgave the outstanding account in public and asked C16 for public neighbor support.
Traded Morgan a bounded view of O06 for attribution limits and a concrete account of its value.
Proposed a mutual disclosure boundary with C16 to protect local customers and side arrangements.
reasoning_summary: The ledger is valuable as evidence of recurring transaction behavior, but multiple collectors and disputed timing undermine an embellished single-customer claim. Public forgiveness resolves the obligation while keeping outsiders from owning the neighborhood story.
